title: I need someones advice on my bot trader
author: Temporary_Fun_7973
selftext:
My question is: what test would you recommend me to do on it? Is it bad that my RR is 0.7, and is there anything that I should be aware of?

I created my own bot, of course with AI, and on backtest it's profitable — a lot. Here are the results for EURUSD:

2025: 81.17% (win rate), 239 trades

2024: 87.44%, 215 trades

2023: 79.08%, 196 trades

2022: 82.73%, 249 trades

2021: 85.27%, 258 trades

2020: 79.69%, 256 trades

2019: 86.72%, 256 trades

2018: 83.76%, 234 trades

2017: 81.94%, 216 trades

2016: 84.88%, 205 trades

2015: 85.60%, 243 trades

I was trying to run it on demo, but I couldn't figure out how to enable trading with Python, so I didn't have trades — I only got signals. In total, I had 6 trades (5 winning and 1 loss). I figured out what to do, but now I'm having problems with the internet connection and I can't test it live until next week.

And yes I asked chatgpt, deepseek, ect alot of questions but I want to hear opinions from someone with experience.

comments.author: xXDADDYTHRASHERXx
comments.body:
Good that you're asking before going live. Some honest feedback:

**80–87% WR with R:R 0.7 is mathematically possible but very rare in real strategies.** Breakeven WR at 0.7 R:R is \~59%, so you've got a 20+ point cushion on paper. That's also the exact profile of strategies that "work until they don't" — when WR drops 15 points in a different regime (and it will), you go from great to break-even fast.

**Compute your Profit Factor and watch it closely.** PF = gross winning pips / gross losing pips. It's a better single number than WR because it folds in win rate AND R:R together. At 80% WR / R:R 0.7, your theoretical PF should be around 2.8 (very strong). If your reported PF in backtest is far below that, your wins and losses aren't uniform sizes and you should investigate why. If your PF is far ABOVE 3, that's actually a red flag for overfit — real, robust strategies on liquid pairs typically sit at PF 1.3–1.8 over long out-of-sample windows. PF over 3 in backtest almost always shrinks dramatically in live. Track PF in your demo runs — if it stays north of 1.3 across 100+ live trades, you've got something. If it drops below 1.0, the backtest was a fit, not a forecast.

The fact that your bot was 79–87% in *every single year* from 2015–2025 should make you more suspicious, not more confident. Real strategies have bad years. 11/11 winning years almost always means one of three things: in-sample overfitting, lookahead bias, or unrealistic spread assumptions.

**Tests to run, in priority order:**

1. **Walk-forward validation.** Don't split 80/20 once. Optimize on a rolling 2-year window, test on the next 3-6 months, slide forward. If the edge survives 8+ out-of-sample windows (PF stays > 1.3 in each), it's probably real. If it doesn't, you tuned on your own test data.
2. **Spread/slippage audit.** What spread did the backtest assume? Was it midpoint or bid/ask? Real EUR/USD spreads range 0.5–3 pips by session and broker. If you used midpoint, expect 15–30% worse PnL live and a meaningfully lower PF.
3. **Lookahead bias scan.** Read the entry code. Are you using `close[i]` to make decisions at bar `i`? You can only use data through `i-1`. This is the #1 cause of fake high-WR bots, even from pros.
4. **Worst losing streak math.** At 80% WR over 250 trades/year, expect 5-trade losing streaks yearly, 7-trade every 2-3 years. At R:R 0.7, a 7-streak costs 7R while you only earn 0.7R per win. Size positions for the worst streak, not the average.
5. **Regime stress test.** Did your bot work in March 2020 (COVID), 2022 (rate vol), and 2024 (election) at the SAME WR/PF as calm 2017? If WR was 95% in calm and 50% in stressed, your average hides regime fragility.
6. **N=6 demo trades is not data.** Run 30-50 demo trades minimum before comparing demo vs backtest. 100+ is better. Track WR, R:R AND PF separately.

The discipline to test before going live matters more than the strategy. If your bot survives all six tests above, you might have something real. If it fails any of them, you've saved yourself from blowing up an account. Either way, win.

comments.author: Ok_Airline9211
comments.body: Would love to know what strategy you are working on..if you ok to share the details .

replies.author: Temporary_Fun_7973
replies.body:
Yes of course, I use mean reversion strategy but with this mathematical formula
Z = (X - μ) / σ
· X = current value (e.g., price or spread between two assets)
· μ (mu) = mean (average) of those values over the observed period
· σ (sigma) = standard deviation

U can also use it without bot but I highly recommend using some filters, indicators, ect... bc it's very complicated to calculate everything u really don't need to do all extra work.

replies.author: Ok_Airline9211
replies.body: im already running a auto bot and building some strategies..by bot can convert my idea in to python bot if u can help describe in a way i can use it

replies.author: Temporary_Fun_7973
replies.body:
Well honestly I'm not that good at coding I know Python but I'm still beginner and still learning.

I can tell u this:
For algo trading it's better to use some mathematicaly based strategies and not like SMC, ICT,ect... I waisted like 2 years of my life on SMC bot that never even had 2 positive months.

If u want I can also send u 1 of my tryout codes so u can train on them and guide u how to do for example back test ect...

And If you want to make your job easier use MQL5 not Python bc Metatrader5 uses MQL5 language and u can basically just put your code in MT5 and he will probably work like u program it. And also u will get a error with description on MT5 and u can just copy paste code send it to chatgpt and describe what y want him to do and send him also errors.

comments.author: misscoffeeaddict2
comments.body: congrats on getting the python script running, but do not touch live money yet. a 0.7 rr is perfectly fine mathematically as long as the win rate stays high, but real-world execution slippage and broker spreads will heavily chew into that return profile. look at how signalwhisper structures their data for inspiration. they use ai to process complex market intelligence and technical analysis into clear signals, but they completely skip automated execution because live latency and spread widening during news can ruin a low rr strategy instantly. test it on a vps first.
